analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet asserts that both Han migrant workers and Uyghurs work in Xinjiang for good income and that Uyghurs have the right to pursue a better life through work, implying voluntariness and normalcy of employment in Xinjiang. Evaluation by narrative: CH1 (West immoral/hostile): Not present; no mention of the West or hostility. CH2 (China as benevolent power internationally): Not applicable; this is domestic, not about international cooperation or multilateralism. CH3 (epic history): Not referenced. CH4 (appropriate political system and honorable values): Indirectly supported—by emphasizing rights, equality of opportunity, and moral legitimacy of work policies toward minorities, it promotes the idea that China’s domestic governance respects rights and is morally upright. CH5 (CCP succeeds): Partially implied—portrays successful socioeconomic management in Xinjiang (good income, opportunities for all ethnicities), suggesting effective governance and harmonious minority coexistence. CH6 (culture/nature/heritage): Not relevant. Conclusion: The tweet promotes CH4 and CH5.
